blurb: Paul's exasperated question cuts through religious pretense to expose the heart of the matter: we who began in the freedom of faith are now trying to perfect ourselves through the very law that once held us captive. The Spirit came not through our careful observance of rules but through the simple hearing of grace, yet how quickly we forget and return to the exhausting work of self-improvement. What folly to think we can complete by human effort what the Father began by divine gift—as if the same hand that raised Christ from the dead suddenly needed our assistance to finish what he started.

verses.text: O foolish Galatians, who hath bewitched you, that ye should not obey the truth, before whose eyes Jesus Christ hath been evidently set forth, crucified among you?
This only would I learn of you, Received ye the Spirit by the works of the law, or by the hearing of faith?
Are ye so foolish? having begun in the Spirit, are ye now made perfect by the flesh?
Have ye suffered so many things in vain? if it be yet in vain.
